old_claim.note: Dry Compass Navigation provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because the node is now scoped to Petty's double-bottomed hull design and sailing trials; dry-compass navigation is not sourced as a prerequisite for the vessel architecture.

source_shape.source_locator: Royal Society Picture Library catalog entry for Double-bottomed ship: the object history records review of the structure and sailing of Petty's new ship, not compass navigation.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source documents Petty's double-bottomed ship as a structural and sailing experiment reviewed by the Royal Society.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Dry-compass navigation may matter to wider seafaring, but the cited evidence supports the vessel's hull and sailing scope without making compass navigation a direct dependency.

ontology_before: Double-Hulled Ship Designs depended directly on dry_compass_navigation, implying compass navigation enabled the specialized hull form.
ontology_after: Petty Double-Bottomed Ship depends directly on sailing; dry-compass navigation is excluded from the scoped hull-design dependency set.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: double_hulled_ship_designs no longer directly depends on dry_compass_navigation.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to a navigation practice rather than Petty's vessel architecture.
A stronger source showed Petty's paired-hull design specifically required dry-compass navigation.
The dry_compass_navigation node were broadened away from compass navigation into all sailing practice.
short_reason: Dry-compass navigation is not evidenced as a direct prerequisite for the hull experiment.
